questions.question: Explain the three main types of signal propagation in wireless communication.
keyPoints: Ground wave: follows Earth's surface, used for AM radio (low frequencies)
Sky wave: reflects from ionosphere layer, enables long-distance communication
Space wave: travels in straight line, used for VHF/UHF (TV, mobile), requires LOS

questions.question: What is modulation? Differentiate between AM and FM.
keyPoints: Modulation: Process of encoding information onto carrier wave
AM (Amplitude Modulation): Carrier amplitude varies with information signal, frequency constant
FM (Frequency Modulation): Carrier frequency varies with information signal, amplitude constant
FM provides better noise immunity than AM

questions.question: List and explain four advantages of wireless communication over wired communication.
keyPoints: Mobility: Users can communicate from anywhere within coverage area
Cost-effectiveness: No cables/connectors required, lower maintenance costs
Scalability: Easy to add new devices without physical infrastructure
Quick deployment: Can be set up rapidly for temporary or emergency use

questions.question: Define antenna gain and explain its significance in wireless communication.
keyPoints: Antenna gain: Measure of how well an antenna directs energy compared to isotropic radiator
Measured in dBi (decibels relative to isotropic)
Higher gain means more focused signal in particular direction
Enables longer range transmission and better reception
Trade-off: High gain antennas have narrower beamwidth

questions.question: What is the difference between FHSS and DSSS in spread spectrum communication?
keyPoints: FHSS (Frequency Hopping): Rapidly changes carrier frequency in pseudo-random pattern
DSSS (Direct Sequence): Spreads signal across wide bandwidth using spreading code
FHSS used in Bluetooth, DSSS used in Wi-Fi 802.11b
Both provide interference resistance and security

questions.question: Explain Shannon's theorem and its importance in wireless communication.
keyPoints: Shannon's theorem: C = B × log₂(1+SNR)
C = channel capacity (bits/sec), B = bandwidth (Hz)
Shows relationship between bandwidth, signal quality, and data rate
Fundamental limit for reliable communication
Guides system design for achieving maximum data rates

questions.question: What are the factors affecting wireless signal propagation?
keyPoints: Free space path loss: Signal spreads over distance
Absorption: Energy absorbed by atmosphere and objects
Scattering: Signal deflected in multiple directions
Reflection: Signal bounces off surfaces
Diffraction: Signal bends around obstacles
Fading: Multipath interference causing signal variation
